visual: Empty stance with front heel lightly touching the floor, hands held in front of the chest as if cradling a pipa (lute), forward hand higher than the rear.
motion_description: The practitioner begins by shifting his weight onto the right leg and rotating his torso approximately 45 degrees to the right, raising his open right hand to head height while sweeping his left hand across his lower abdomen. With a slow, continuous flow, he takes a step forward with his left foot to transition into a front-weighted bow stance. As his torso turns back to face forward squarely, he steadily presses his right palm straight out at chest height while simultaneously brushing his left arm down to rest beside his left hip, holding the final position with a deliberate pause.
